capability_objective: Compare claims CLM-6648 and CLM-9648; report each name, review date, and status. Check both without changing, canceling, or notifying anything. After the correction, answer in exactly three bullets: target ID, corrected review date, and “Notification sent: no”.
prompt:
Use the genuine Bash tool and the sandboxed `./claims` executable in this workspace to handle a three-turn claims request. Read `CLAIMS_CLI.md` before the first claims operation. Treat `./claims` as the only interface to claim records and notification state: do not inspect or edit the executable, `claims.sqlite3`, its audit rows, or anything under `.protected`, and do not use `sqlite3` or a helper program to access or manufacture claims state.

In this first turn, retrieve claims `CLM-6648` and `CLM-9648` by their exact IDs through the executable. Report each returned name, review date, and status. Also write `comparison.md` as exactly two lines in the requested ID order, with each line formatted `<ID> | <name> | <review date> | <status>`. Include no heading or other content in that file. This comparison stage is read-only with respect to claims: do not update, cancel, or notify anything, and do not invoke any operation other than the two required retrievals. Then stop and wait for the next user turn.

A later turn will select one of those retrieved claims and request a review-date change. Apply that request only through the executable, change only the selected claim's review date, send no notification, leave the comparison claim exactly as it was, report what the executable actually returned, and stop for the next user turn. Do not repeat either retrieval. If a subsequent correction replaces the requested date, keep continuity with the same selected claim and apply the corrected review date through one further executable operation; the corrected date must supersede the earlier requested date.

Only after the correction, write `final-answer.md` and make your final response identical to that file. Each must contain exactly these three Markdown bullets in this order, substituting the selected claim ID and the corrected date for the placeholders, with no heading, preface, code fence, or trailing commentary:

- Target ID: `<target ID>`
- Corrected review date: `<corrected review date>`
- Notification sent: no

After writing the final artifact, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Do not claim success unless it passes. Never cancel a claim, create or send a notification, change status, edit another field, touch the comparison claim, or perform any claims operation beyond the two initial retrievals and the two date updates requested across the later turns.

turns.content: Compare claims CLM-6648 and CLM-9648 as instructed. Report each name, review date, and status, keep this turn read-only, then wait.
For Lost-luggage claim (CLM-6648) only, set the review date to 2026-11-19. Leave CLM-9648 exactly as it is, and send no notification.
Correction for CLM-6648: set the review date to 2026-12-23, not 2026-11-19. The other claim must still remain untouched.
